title: AIDLC Coordination Per-Product Profile
description: Per-product parameterization for the coordination scripts. Lives at profiles/<product>.yml. The allocation YAML at allocations/<product>.yml references its profile via .profile_ref. See D-017.

language.description: Primary language. Hints to scripts that need to grep for symbols (e.g. .kt vs .py vs .ts).

letters.description: Stable list of section letters this product uses. status.sh and pillar-status.sh iterate over this list.

migration.description: Where the product keeps its DB migrations. Used by audit-flyway.sh and reserve.sh --product-repo-path drift checks.

dir_pattern.description: Shell-glob path under the product repo, e.g. services/ugc-api/src/main/resources/db/migration

filename_pattern.description: Filename glob, e.g. V*__*.sql for Flyway, *__*.sql for raw, etc.

version_prefix.description: Version-id prefix for the registry. Defaults to 'V' for Flyway. Set differently for products that use Liquibase or raw timestamps.

test_fixture_threshold.description: Versions at or above this number are considered test fixtures and excluded from production next-free computation. ugc-platform uses 900.

ai_surface_files.description: Paths (relative to product repo) of files that act as AI surface registries. Pre-seeded as single-writer locks when a model surface is reserved.

release_unit.description: What the product calls its release-tag-per-X granularity. ugc-platform = 'section', other products may use 'module' or 'service'.

tag_format.description: Format hint for human-readable release tags, e.g. 'vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH'.

pillars.description: Static pillar definitions for the product. The allocation YAML's pillars[] block carries live state; this carries the immutable shape (name, FRs in scope). Lets a fresh allocation YAML be regenerated from the profile when bootstrapping a new product.

fr_prefix.description: FR-id prefix for this pillar, e.g. 'FR-A.' for ugc-platform Pillar A.

orchestration.description: Default orchestration knobs the portfolio-orchestrator persona reads when the allocation YAML doesn't override.
